summary: Audience recording with poor sound captures a solid spring‑93 Rosemont show highlighted by fiery Loose Lucy, Sugar Maggs and a standout Black Peter.
review: The concert was an energetic early‑1993 spring‑tour date that delivered several memorable moments – loose, funky “Loose Lucy”, a rollicking “Sugar Maggs”, and a powerful “Black Peter”.  The audience recording is classic Rosemont tape quality – muddy and uneven – which detracts from the listening experience, but fans still recommend it for the strong performances.

ai_rating.rationale: Show is generally praised with many standout songs (≈4/5) while the audience recording is consistently described as poor (≈2/5); weighted combination yields ~3.4.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Classic Rosemont audience tape – muffled, occasional drop‑outs and low clarity; reviewers call it mediocre or pretty poor.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans note a solid, lengthy set with high energy despite heavy security and bad weather; the first set had some flubs (ball‑dropping) but the band recovered and closed strong.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for tighter playing and blistering solos on Loose Lucy, Sunshine and other jams.
band_member_comments.Bob: Recognized for redeeming himself with a great Sugar Maggs performance.
band_member_comments.Drums: One reviewer disliked moments when drums were cut out, feeling the missing space hurt the flow.
